preamble: In the 'guttural' rich phonologies of Semitic languages, low vowels like [a] are articulatory neighbors to pharyngeal consonants. A strengthening (fortition) of a low vowel can lead to its realization as a pharyngeal fricative [ʕ].

examples.note: Low vowels are often retracted and strengthened when near other pharyngeals, occasionally leading to consonant-vowel mergers.

sources: Watson, J. C. E. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic. Oxford University Press.
